title: Ignoring Small Expenses
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you track small daily expenses without making budgeting overly burdensome?
What strategies help maintain awareness of small expenses while still enjoying life?

options.label: Add up over the month
options.reflection: Exactly! Small daily expenses add up quickly over the course of a month, potentially representing a significant portion of your budget.

options.label: Don't matter
options.reflection: Actually, small daily spends do matter. Even small amounts can accumulate significantly over time.

options.label: Save you money in the long run
options.reflection: Small daily expenses reduce your available funds rather than saving money in the long run.

options.label: Provide good investment opportunities
options.reflection: Small daily expenses are typically consumption rather than investments that generate returns.

stages.prompt: What is the cumulative effect of spending ₹20 daily on tea/coffee?

options.label: ₹600 per month (minimal impact)
options.reflection: Actually, ₹20 daily would be ₹600 per month assuming 30 days, which can represent a significant portion of a young person's budget.

options.label: Insignificant amount
options.reflection: ₹600 per month is a significant sum that could be redirected toward savings or other important goals.

options.label: Zero impact on finances
options.reflection: Any spending, regardless of size, has an impact on your overall financial position.

options.label: ₹600 per month (significant impact)
options.reflection: Perfect! ₹20 daily adds up to ₹600 per month, which is a significant amount that could be allocated elsewhere.

stages.prompt: Why do people often ignore small recurring expenses?

options.label: They are always necessary expenses
options.reflection: Many small recurring expenses are actually discretionary, not necessary, such as snacks, drinks, or entertainment.

options.label: They have higher priority than big expenses
options.reflection: Big expenses are typically prioritized over small ones, but small expenses often add up to be substantial.

options.label: They seem too small to notice individually
options.reflection: Exactly! Small expenses seem insignificant when viewed individually, causing people to overlook their cumulative impact.

options.label: They provide significant financial benefits
options.reflection: Small recurring expenses typically provide personal satisfaction rather than financial benefits.

stages.prompt: What happens when you ignore small daily expenses in budgeting?

options.label: Budgets become simpler to manage
options.reflection: While excluding small expenses might simplify the budget, it also makes it inaccurate and prone to failure.

options.label: Budgets become inaccurate and fail
options.reflection: Perfect! Ignoring small daily expenses makes budgets inaccurate and likely to fail, as these expenses accumulate significantly over time.

options.label: Budgets are more likely to succeed
options.reflection: Ignoring small expenses actually makes budgets less likely to succeed due to unaccounted spending.

options.label: Budgets have more flexibility
options.reflection: Untracked small expenses reduce flexibility by consuming budgeted funds unexpectedly.

stages.prompt: How can tracking small expenses improve your financial health?

options.label: Creates awareness of actual spending patterns
options.reflection: Exactly! Tracking small expenses creates awareness of actual spending patterns and helps identify areas where money can be redirected to better uses.

options.label: Makes financial management more complex
options.reflection: While tracking adds some complexity, it provides valuable insights that improve financial management.

options.label: Restricts your ability to enjoy life
options.reflection: Tracking small expenses doesn't restrict enjoyment; it helps ensure you have enough for both necessities and pleasures.

options.label: Creates inequality in spending
options.reflection: Tracking expenses doesn't create inequality; it helps ensure balanced and intentional spending.
